objective: Validate the hardened MCP-first Samwise session review workflow and confirm that it fails closed when live PersonaKit MCP transport is unavailable.

details.blockedReason: Live PersonaKit MCP calls returned `Transport closed` during verification.

objective: Review the Samwise persona, associated kits, and all Samwise sessions as one stack-level MCP-first red-pen pass focused on whether context is scaling cleanly through kits and sessions.

topFindings: samwise-orchestration-core is still too broad and reintroduces planning, hiring, and retrospective ballast into execution and oversight sessions.
samwise-coffee-checkpoint still pulls planning contracts directly into a wake-up flow that is supposed to stay lightweight.
The session directory still marks several Samwise specialist lanes as candidate even though continuity records treat them as validated workflows.

highestRiskByArtifact.samwise-orchestration-core: One orchestration kit still stands in for planning, hiring, oversight, and retrospective concerns.
highestRiskByArtifact.samwise-coffee-checkpoint: Wake-up flow is heavier than promised because planning contracts live directly in the intent.
highestRiskByArtifact.session-directory: Lifecycle labels understate mature Samwise specialist lanes and can surprise operators.

details.highestValueNextStep: Decompose samwise-orchestration-core so planning, oversight, and retrospective context are loaded explicitly instead of through one broad shared kit.

objective: Review the full Samwise-related pack graph as one MCP-first red-pen pass focused on whether shared kits, directives, intents, essentials, and Orbit-specific packs scale cleanly through Samwise sessions.

topFindings: samwise-orchestration-core is still the dominant scope-bleed source because it bundles planning, hiring, worktree gating, logging, and retrospective contracts into one shared override.
The worktree execution layer is effective but not cohesive because authority is split across the orchestration kit, Samwise oversight directive, delivery intent, and gating essential.
orbit-rerun-squad-execution still leaves specialist session routes optional even though the directive expects explicit session mapping before coding begins.

highestRiskByArtifact.samwise-orchestration-core: One broad orchestration kit still re-broadens several distinct workflow families.
highestRiskByArtifact.gated-worktree-delivery: Delivery entry mixes gate, evidence, and retrospective concerns that should not all ride together by default.
highestRiskByArtifact.orbit-rerun-squad-execution: Session routing is still under-enforced at the intent layer.

details.highestValueNextStep: Decompose samwise-orchestration-core into planning, worktree-oversight, and retrospective successors, then harden orbit-rerun-squad-execution so specialist session routes are required or deterministic.
